concerns.text: T020 references the literal string '[deferred]' in the hold-out reservation logic: 'Reserve [deferred] of samples'. FR-006 and the spec require reserving a specific fraction (defined as HOLD_OUT_FRACTION in T004). Using the placeholder text as a logic parameter creates an unexecutable task that fails to satisfy the FR-006 constraint of reserving a defined set before feature selection.

T022 includes a note stating 'Run as a mandatory diagnostic report per FR-012 despite... the plan's preference for SHAP' and 'VIF is a category error'. While the task exists, the explicit contradiction in the task description against the plan's reasoning creates a high risk of implementation refusal or semantic weakening of FR-012. The task must be rewritten to affirmatively mandate VIF calculation as a required diagnostic per FR-012, removing the 'despite' language that suggests the requirement is an exception to the plan's logic.

T021 describes applying FDR correction to 'pairwise metabolite-resistance correlations' but omits the critical constraint 'computed on the training fold only' found in FR-008 and the spec. Without this explicit restriction in the task description, the implementation risks computing correlations on the full dataset (including the hold-out set), violating the data independence constraint (Constitution Principle VII) and FR-008.

T020 specifies 'max_depth=10' and mentions 'GridSearchCV' but fails to explicitly define the search range for the 'tunable up to 20' constraint in FR-005. The task description must explicitly state the GridSearchCV parameter grid includes values up to 20 (e.g., 'max_depth range [10, 20]') to ensure the deliverable satisfies the full scope of FR-005.
